source.title: Personnel Handling Incident - Man Rider Uncontrolled Descent

context.region: Gulf of America, Green Canyon Block 432

actions_taken: Employee evacuated via medivac to onshore medical facility
Joint investigation conducted with winch manufacturer technicians
BSEE onsite investigation performed on February 24, 2025
event.summary: On February 17, 2025, an Expro Americas Inc. employee was using a man rider on the Noble Globetrotter 1 drillship to access the Coil Tubing Lift Frame at approximately 100 feet to tighten a leaking hydraulic hose. After completing the task, the man rider began descending at an accelerated uncontrolled rate. The hoist operator attempted to stop the descent by moving controls to hoist position and reaching for the emergency stop, but the employee reached the rig floor before the stop could be activated. Investigation revealed the OEM Programming and Control Logic had been altered from Default to Warning settings, bypassing critical safety systems including slack line detection, travel limits, and encoder performance monitoring. This prevented the load brakes from engaging. Environmental degradation of the drive motor and encoder, inadequate preventive maintenance, and insufficient third-party inspections were contributing factors. The employee was medically cleared for full duty.
recommendations: BSEE Houma District has no recommendations for the Office of Incident Investigations at this time

hazards.name: Suspended Load - Personnel in Man Rider

threats.name: Unauthorized Modification of Safety Control Logic
Environmental Deterioration of Drive Motor and Encoder

controls.name: OEM Programming and Control Logic (PCL) Default Settings
controls.barrier_role: Prevent unauthorized changes to safety-critical control systems
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineered safety system to prevent unsafe operating conditions

supporting_text: The investigation revealed that the original equipment manufacture's (OEM's) Programing and Control Logic (PCL) was altered at some point
The original equipment manufacture's (OEM's) programing changed from 'Default' to 'Warning' settings
This allowed three separate system faults: Slack line, Upper / Lower Travel Limit, and Encoder performance / speed resolution to be overridden

controls.name: PCL Password Protection and Memory Storage
controls.barrier_role: Prevent unauthorized access to safety-critical programming
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to restrict access to safety systems

supporting_text: Due to the nature of PCL not having memory storage or password protection, it is unknown as to who or when the logic was altered

controls.name: Slack Line Detection System
controls.barrier_role: Detect and prevent slack line conditions during lifting operations
controls.lod_basis: Automated safety system to detect unsafe operating conditions

supporting_text: This allowed three separate system faults: Slack line, Upper / Lower Travel Limit, and Encoder performance / speed resolution to be overridden

controls.name: Upper/Lower Travel Limit System
controls.barrier_role: Prevent over-travel of man rider beyond safe limits
controls.lod_basis: Automated safety system to prevent unsafe positioning

supporting_text: This allowed three separate system faults: Slack line, Upper / Lower Travel Limit, and Encoder performance / speed resolution to be overridden

controls.name: Encoder Performance and Speed Resolution Monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Monitor and control descent/ascent speed within safe parameters
controls.lod_basis: Automated monitoring system for safe operation

supporting_text: This allowed three separate system faults: Slack line, Upper / Lower Travel Limit, and Encoder performance / speed resolution to be overridden
The investigation team also noted environmentally induced deterioration of the drive motor and encoder

controls.name: Load Brake System
controls.barrier_role: Automatically engage brakes to stop uncontrolled descent
controls.lod_basis: Secondary safety system to arrest unsafe motion

supporting_text: This also prevented the control logic from engaging either of the two load brakes
Because of this bypass, the brakes did not engage allowing AP to be lowered uncontrolled

controls.name: Hoist Operator Manual Control
controls.barrier_role: Manual operator intervention to control descent rate
controls.lod_basis: Human operator as backup to automated systems

supporting_text: According to the hoist operator's witness statement, the first action he took was to move the control to the hoist position, but the man rider continued to descend at the same rate
This may have contributed to the hoist continuing to descend when the hoist operator went to neutral and when he hoisted up

controls.name: Emergency Stop Button
controls.barrier_role: Immediate manual shutdown of lifting operation

supporting_text: The hoist operator stated he attempted to hit the emergency stop, but AP was on the floor before the button could be pressed
The emergency stop was not activated during the event

controls.name: Preventive Maintenance Program
controls.barrier_role: Maintain equipment integrity and functionality through scheduled maintenance
controls.lod_basis: Proactive maintenance to prevent equipment degradation

supporting_text: Lack of preventive maintenance by the contractor and insufficient inspections performed by subcontracted third-party inspectors were found to be contributing factors
Although the contractor has a preventative maintenance schedule in place, the final report pointed out lack of maintenance as another factor
Equipment failure- inadequate preventive maintenance

controls.name: Third-Party Equipment Inspections
controls.barrier_role: Independent verification of equipment condition and safety

supporting_text: Insufficient inspections performed by subcontracted third-party inspectors were found to be contributing factors
Equipment failure- inadequate preventive maintenance. Insufficient inspections performed by a subcontracted third-party agency
The lack of maintenance and lack of attention during pre-use inspections led to the failure of the hoist operators to identify problems associated with the safety and functionality of the man rider
The environmental degradation of the man rider should have been observed during maintenance checks and pre-use inspections
Inadequate supervision on maintenance checks and pre-use inspections

controls.name: Control of Work (COW) Permit System
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper planning, authorization, and control of high-risk work
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control for work authorization and coordination

supporting_text: A Control of Work meeting (COW) was issued for a 'Man riding – Derrick (work at Height)' task
Neither the winch operator nor AP were specified in the COW
Although the Tour Pusher attended the transition to work meeting, he was not listed in the COW

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify and mitigate hazards before work begins
controls.lod_basis: Pre-task hazard identification and risk assessment

supporting_text: A Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was completed and reviewed by Noble's Tour Pusher, Noble's banksman, hoist operator, and the AP

controls.name: Visual Contact Requirement - Hoist Operator
controls.barrier_role: Maintain continuous visual monitoring of personnel during lifting

supporting_text: The COW permit included a control to ensure the winch operator maintained visual with the person man riding at all times and prevented blind lifts
The winch control console was positioned at an elevated location that obstructed the operator's view of the CTLF

controls.name: Tour Pusher Supervision of Man Riding Operations
controls.barrier_role: Provide direct supervision during high-risk lifting operations

supporting_text: The Tour Pusher should supervise man riding operations as per contractor policy but was not present during the event
Supervision- Inadequate supervision on maintenance checks and pre-use inspections

controls.name: Secondary Fall Protection (Personal Fall Arrest System)
controls.barrier_role: Protect personnel from fall consequences if primary system fails
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense personal protective equipment

supporting_text: No secondary fall protection was worn by AP

controls.name: Medical Evacuation and Treatment
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury severity through rapid medical response

supporting_text: AP did not appear injured but as a precaution he was sent to an onshore medical facility via medivac for further evaluation and was cleared for full duty

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
